questions.question: Solve |2x - 5| = 11.
questions.explanation: Split into two cases: 2x - 5 = 11 gives x = 8; and 2x - 5 = -11 gives x = -3. Both are valid solutions.

questions.question: Solve |x + 3| ≤ 7.
questions.explanation: For |A| ≤ B, write -B ≤ A ≤ B. So -7 ≤ x + 3 ≤ 7. Subtract 3 from all parts to get -10 ≤ x ≤ 4.

questions.question: A thermometer reading differs from the actual temperature by at most 0.3°F. If the thermometer reads 72°F, what is the range of actual temperatures?
questions.explanation: The absolute difference is |T - 72| ≤ 0.3. This gives -0.3 ≤ T - 72 ≤ 0.3, so 71.7 ≤ T ≤ 72.3.

questions.question: Solve |3x - 4| = |x + 2|.
questions.explanation: When absolute values are equal, either 3x - 4 = x + 2 (giving x = 3) or 3x - 4 = -(x + 2) (giving 4x = 2, so x = 1/2).

questions.question: Solve |x - 6| > 4.
questions.explanation: For |A| > B, split into A > B or A < -B. So x - 6 > 4 gives x > 10, and x - 6 < -4 gives x < 2.

questions.question: Solve 2|x + 1| - 3 = 5.
questions.explanation: First isolate the absolute value: 2|x + 1| = 8, so |x + 1| = 4. Then x + 1 = 4 gives x = 3, and x + 1 = -4 gives x = -5.
